title: The Almshouse
introText: March 1841. You have been appointed keeper of the town almshouse. Among the paupers, orphans, and infirm you will find men and women the town calls 'lunatics' — some kept in cages or chains in the cellar. A schoolteacher named Miss Dix is touring such institutions across the Commonwealth. She has asked to visit in thirty days.

epilogueText.damning: Miss Dix's report to the Legislature named your almshouse among those she called 'a spectacle of suffering.' The town selectmen replaced you within the month.
epilogueText.mixed: Miss Dix's report acknowledged improvement but noted the work was only half begun. Your appointment was renewed, conditionally.
epilogueText.commendation: Miss Dix praised the almshouse as an example to neighboring towns. Her 1843 Memorial to the Legislature would cite such reforms as proof that humane care was possible.
